report_name: ShinyHunters Breach at Canada Life Exposes Data of 70,000 Customers

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A well-known extortion group that perpetrated a cyberattack against The Canada Life Assurance Company, gaining access via a compromised employee account and exfiltrating the personal information of up to 70,000 individuals. They threatened to leak the data on a dark web forum if a ransom was not paid.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and extortion for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gained initial access to Canada Life's internal systems by using the credentials of a single compromised employee account.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access, the attackers accessed and collected sensitive information on 70,000 individuals from internal data repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated the collected customer data from Canada Life's network to their own controlled servers.

identities.name: The Canada Life Assurance Company
identities.description: A major Canadian insurance provider that was the victim of a data breach by the ShinyHunters group, which exposed the personal information of up to 70,000 individuals.

identities.name: Zara
identities.description: Mentioned as another company compromised by ShinyHunters as part of a larger campaign.

identities.name: 7-Eleven
identities.description: Mentioned as another company compromised by ShinyHunters as part of a larger campaign.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Forum
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum where ShinyHunters publicly claimed the attack on Canada Life, posted threats, and set a ransom deadline.
